artifacts.content:
Hi Ana,

Finally sitting down after getting Diego through his Grant Beacon math homework and chasing Camila into the shower. I keep thinking about how much you’ve been holding me up these last couple of years, so I wanted to write it out instead of just texting “gracias” every time you send money.

When I fell three months behind on rent at Cedar in late 2023, I honestly thought we were going to lose the apartment. You stepping in like my informal “financial guardian,” talking to the landlord at 1674 W Cedar and getting him to agree to that $200‑per‑month payment plan in January 2024, probably kept us from ending up in some motel. And then since January 2024 you’ve basically been plugging the holes every month with those extra $200 transfers from your MA job in Aurora whenever I’m short because of unpaid time off.

It’s all connected to everything else. Remember that fainting episode at Front Range in October 2023, after the doctor at Denver Health warned in September that my blood pressure was “dangerously high”? Papá started driving down from Greeley to stay overnight some weeks, and I kept missing hours between his appointments and my panic attacks. Then in March 2024 the attacks at work got so bad I ended up with Dr. Karen Phillips at the Denver Health Outpatient Behavioral Health Clinic every other Thursday at 6:00 p.m., and she put me on 50 mg sertraline on April 18. Half the time I can’t sleep before early‑morning calls, which doesn’t help with getting to the warehouse on time.

Anyway, all of that lands on you too, and I know it. I’m trying so hard to get stable so you don’t have to keep rescuing me. I started that little spreadsheet again—rent, car, the $275 for Diego’s hospital bill, everything—so at least I can see it clearly.

Te quiero mucho,
Maria

Hola Elena,

Los niños se acaban de ir con Javier for his every‑other‑Wednesday pick‑up at 6:00, so the apartment is finally quiet. I’m heating up Mami’s pozole and trying not to replay every awful work moment in my head, but of course my brain goes straight back there.

Do you remember when you were still at Front Range and my anxiety was just starting to get bad? It feels like it’s been building since your own breakdown back in March 2023 in that warehouse office on W Alameda, when you called me from the HR conference room shaking. Ever since then you’ve basically been my emergency contact for panic stuff. And then this year just piled on: the panic episode in the HR disciplinary meeting on March 7, 2024, when I started hyperventilating in that same conference room; the March 12 logistics team meeting in Conference Room B where my supervisor and the warehouse manager double‑teamed me for almost an hour about that $14,800 shipment error until I froze and couldn’t answer simple questions.

On top of that, Dr. Karen Phillips at Denver Health’s Outpatient Behavioral Health Clinic has me on 50 mg sertraline since April 18, 2024, and between that and the weekly CBT I just started with Karen Liu at the Denver Health Osage Street Clinic in January, my head feels like mush after 3:00 p.m. Dr. Alvarez warned me about the drowsiness when he adjusted the meds in late November 2023, but I didn’t realize how much it would mess with performance reviews, like that December 5, 2023 one where I missed half the details.

Meanwhile Javier’s still using every little thing against me in the custody mess—Laura Gutierrez keeps reminding me that anything in my records could show up in Courtroom 10M for Case No. 2023DR01492. No wonder my chest feels tight half the time.

Anyway, enough ranting. Tell me how Albuquerque is treating you and if your mom’s knee is any better.

Un abrazo fuerte,
Maria

Personal timeline & reminders – health, money, kids

– April 18, 2022 – custody mediation at Denver County Courthouse. Javier raised his voice, I had a full‑on anxiety attack in the mediation room and they had to stop for 20 minutes. Ever since then, I can feel my body shut down when more than one person is talking at me. If I’m going into any stressful meeting, I need Ana or Elena on speaker so they can take notes and keep me grounded.

– May 2023 – Papá’s minor stroke in Greeley. He started staying with me off and on at Cedar. He was in the living room that night when my supervisor called yelling about my FMLA days; he heard everything. Then June 3, 2024, he had that mild stroke at North Colorado Medical Center, and now I’m driving the 140‑mile round trip to Greeley at least two Saturdays a month to help with his meds and PT.

– September 2022 – Diego’s asthma hospitalization at Children’s. I’m still on the hook for that $9,800, plus the $4,700 CareCredit from his follow‑ups and Camila’s dental work at Alpine Dental Kids in February 2024. With the wage garnishment that started in May 2024 and the $275/month payment, I cannot miss a full day of work.

– January 9, 2024 – landlord came to the door around 8:30 p.m., said “eviction” in front of Diego over the $840 past‑due rent. Don’t ever let it get that close again.

Breathe. One thing at a time: keep hours steady at Front Range, keep Javier calm enough to stick to the every‑other‑Wednesday and weekend pickups, protect therapy time, and sleep when I can.

Event: Therapy + meds – Dr. Phillips
Date: Thursday, 2024-06-20
Time: 6:00–6:45 p.m.
Location: Denver Health Outpatient Behavioral Health Clinic
Attendees: Me

Notes:
– Since March 2024 panic attacks at Front Range got worse (especially after that March 7 HR meeting where I started hyperventilating in the conference room), I’ve been coming here every other Thursday at 6:00 p.m. with Dr. Karen Phillips.
– Review how I’m doing on 50 mg sertraline (started April 18, 2024). Still drowsy after 3:00 p.m.; hard time sleeping the night before early‑morning calls. Ask if we can tweak timing so I’m more awake for Diego’s 504 meetings at Grant Beacon and Camila’s reading conferences at Valverde.
– Talk about ongoing money stress: $9,800 Children’s Hospital Colorado bill from Diego’s September 2022 asthma hospitalization; $4,700 CareCredit from his treatments + Camila’s Alpine Dental Kids work (Feb 2024); $275/month payment; wage garnishment since May 2024.
– Also mention Papá’s June 3, 2024 mild stroke at North Colorado Medical Center in Greeley and the two‑Saturdays‑a‑month drives to help with groceries, meds, and paperwork. Need help setting boundaries so I don’t burn out.
– Parenting/court stress: Case No. 2023DR01492 in Denver District Court with Javier; next hearing at Lindsey‑Flanigan Courthouse, Courtroom 10M. Ask for grounding strategies for the night before.

Reminder: leave work by 4:45 p.m., tell Karen (supervisor) it’s a standing medical appointment. Pack snacks for kids; Ana will cover pickup.

metadata.title: Appt – Dr. Phillips (therapy/meds)
metadata.location: Denver Health Outpatient Behavioral Health Clinic

artifacts.content:
Hi Laura,

I hope you’re doing okay and not buried under too many cases. I wanted to update you a bit before we get closer to the next date in Case No. 2023DR01492 at the Lindsey‑Flanigan Courthouse (Courtroom 10M).

Things with Javier are the usual mix of civil and tense. He’s still doing the every‑other‑Wednesday 6:00 p.m. pick‑ups at my apartment on W Cedar and alternating weekends per the June 2020 Denver County Court parenting plan, but we’ve had more arguments lately about my schedule and money. He keeps bringing up that October 2024 “verbal warning” my supervisor, Mark Ellison, gave me at the Front Range warehouse on W Alameda because I left early twice—once for Diego’s 504 plan meeting at Grant Beacon on October 9 and once for Camila’s reading intervention conference at Valverde Elementary on October 16. He uses that as proof I’m “irresponsable” with work.

What he doesn’t see is everything else going on: Papá’s mild stroke on June 3, 2024 at North Colorado Medical Center in Greeley, the two‑Saturdays‑a‑month I’m driving 140 miles round‑trip to help my parents, the $9,800 Children’s Hospital Colorado bill from Diego’s September 2022 asthma hospitalization and the $4,700 CareCredit from his treatments and Camila’s dental work that are now in collections. With the wage garnishment since May 2024 and the $275 monthly payment, missing even one full day at Front Range could sink us.

On top of that, my anxiety is still rough. I’m with Dr. Karen Phillips at Denver Health’s Outpatient Behavioral Health Clinic every other Thursday at 6:00 p.m., on 50 mg sertraline since April 18, 2024. I still remember that April 18, 2022 mediation when Javier yelled and they had to stop for 20 minutes—my body reacts the same way just thinking about another hearing.

If there’s anything specific I should start gathering or tracking before the next court date, please let me know.

Best,
Maria

Thread with Elena R.

Maria: Oye, are you still awake? Kids just left with Javier for his weekend and my brain is spinning.

Elena: I’m up. What happened?

Maria: It’s everything at once. Remember when you were fired from Front Range back in Oct 2022 after you reported our supervisor for falsifying delivery logs? Sometimes I feel like they learned nothing. Last month Mark Ellison pulled me into his office at the warehouse on W Alameda and gave me a verbal warning for leaving early to go to Diego’s 504 meeting at Grant Beacon (Oct 9) and Camila’s reading conference at Valverde (Oct 16). He said if I “keep putting family over work” it’ll show up in my file.

Elena: Ugh. Same playbook.

Maria: And then there’s home stuff. You were with me at DPD District 4 in February when we filed that report after Javier showed up at Cedar yelling about child support. Now he acts nice in front of the kids but keeps throwing my money problems in my face. Diego still remembers the landlord coming on Jan 9 around 8:30 p.m., threatening eviction over the $840 past‑due rent while he was doing his Grant Beacon math homework at the table.

Elena: Poor Diego. That’s a lot for him.

Maria: Plus Ana basically lives here now, sharing my room since March 2023 after her husband shoved her in front of the kids. Between her helping with Diego and Camila when I’m on late shifts, Papá’s June 3 mini‑stroke in Greeley, and my every‑other‑Thursday 6 p.m. sessions with Dr. Phillips at Denver Health, I feel like our whole family is held together with duct tape.

Elena: You’re doing so much, amiga. Breathe.

Maria: Trying. Thanks for listening.

Money + housing priorities – Cedar Ave

1. Rent/back rent
– I’m still paying off the mess from April–September 2025: $6,300 in back rent on 1674 W Cedar, Apt 204. Carlos Jiménez posted that eviction notice on my door August 3, 2025 and filed in Denver County Court. Goal: get that cleared so there’s no record hanging over us when the lease renews.
– Remember the earlier scare: January 9, 2024, landlord at the door around 8:30 p.m. threatening eviction over the $840 past‑due while Diego was doing Grant Beacon math at the table. Never again.

2. Medical/debt
– Diego’s asthma hospitalization at Children’s Hospital Colorado (Sept 2022): $9,800 balance.
– CareCredit opened Feb 2024 for Diego’s follow‑ups + Camila’s Alpine Dental Kids work: $4,700, now in collections because my hours got cut in March 2025.
– Wage garnishment since May 2024, $275/month. Absolutely cannot miss full days at Front Range.

3. Family obligations
– Papá’s mild stroke June 3, 2024 at North Colorado Medical Center in Greeley. Two Saturdays a month driving 140 miles round‑trip to help with meds, PT, groceries.
– Ana has been sending about $200/month since Jan 2024 and basically acting as my financial backup.

4. Mental health
– Keep every‑other‑Thursday 6:00 p.m. with Dr. Phillips at Denver Health Outpatient Behavioral Health.
– Ask her about how all this money stress ties into the panic attacks at work (especially March 7 HR meeting) and the way I froze during that April 18, 2022 mediation with Javier when they had to pause 20 minutes.

One step at a time. Priority: keep Cedar, keep kids stable, keep my job and my sanity.
